title: Aquatic and Land-Based Therapy for Postural Control in Children with Spastic CP
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"33s7r","text":"This single-center randomized clinical trial aims to evaluate the effectiveness of aquatic therapy and land-based therapy on postural control in children aged 5–12 years with spastic cerebral palsy (CP). Participants will undergo a 12-week program with two 45-minute sessions per week, including warm-up, therapist-guided motor activities and cool-down exercises delivered in a therapeutic pool or on land. The primary outcome is postural control, measured using the Korean-Modified Trunk Control Measurement Scale (K-TCMS). Secondary outcomes include functional mobility and coordination, assessed with the Pediatric Evaluation of Disability Inventory (PEDI). This study seeks to determine the impact of aquatic and land-based therapy on postural control and functional abilities, providing evidence to guide clinical and community-based rehabilitation strategies for children with spastic CP, particularly in resource-limited settings.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"7du5p","text":"Informed Consent: Obtain written informed consent from parents/legal guardians and provide full explanation of study  purpose, procedures, risks, and benefits.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dbfpt","text":"Safety protocols:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5c3a","text":"Aquatic therapy: Conduct in a temperature-controlled pool (32–34°C) with constant supervision.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a6idb","text":"Land-based therapy: Conduct in a safe clinical physiotherapy setting.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6h9kb","text":"Ensure presence of first aid, resuscitation equipment, and trained staff.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5uro","text":"Monitor for discomfort, fatigue, spasticity changes, or adverse reactions.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"beu8d","text":"All sessions supervised by licensed pediatric physiotherapists.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3dnrb","text":"Aquatic therapy conducted with certified aquatic therapist \u0026 lifeguard; flotation aids used as needed.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"34u3n","text":"Only medically cleared children will participate.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2lsqk","text":"Continuous monitoring for fatigue, discomfort, increased spasticity.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"15js8","text":"Fall prevention measures in poolside and land settings.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2c268","text":"Water temperature maintained (32–34°C) with hydration breaks.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3ne3s","text":"Adverse events (e.g., soreness, pain, seizures, respiratory distress) will be recorded and managed.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3nfv6","text":"Emergency equipment and CPR-trained staff available on-site.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"6hn9a","text":"Aquatic Therapy Pool: Shallow, child-safe pool with water temperature maintained at 82–86°F (28–30°C).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"foi2t","text":"Therapeutic Equipment: Kickboards, pool noodles, balls, hula hoops, floating devices, and water toys.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a0ocp","text":"Safety Gear: Life vests or floatation devices, non-slip water shoes, towels, and warm blankets.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"56k0o","text":"Assessment Tools: Korean-Modified Trunk Control Measurement Scale (K-TCMS), Pediatric Evaluation of Disability Inventory (PEDI) and parent feedback forms.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7n7c5","text":"Therapist Materials: Whistle, stopwatch, first aid kit, waterproof writing pads.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"furhb","text":"Health and Comfort: Hydration supplies, sunscreen (if outdoor), extra clothing, and dry towels.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"983o8","text":"Documentation: Consent forms, forms.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"85bvn","text":"Emergency Equipment: Life-saving devices (e.g., rescue buoy), communication devices, poolside safety\nsignage.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
